Documentation

🚀Getting Started📦Installation🔄Transactions & Async✏️CRUD Operations🔍Querying🤖Source Generators📡Change Data Capture🌍Spatial Indexing🏗️Architecture🆔Custom ID Converters⚡Benchmarks🔌Schema-less API🔎BLQL📈TimeSeries🗝️Key-Value Store

🏗️ Architecture

Deep dive into BLite's internal architecture, storage engine, and BSON format.

Storage Engine

BLite uses a page-based storage engine with memory-mapped files:

  • PageFile - Memory-mapped file manager with block-aligned growth
  • Write-Ahead Log (WAL) - Durability and crash recovery
  • SlottedPage - Variable-length record storage
  • B-Tree Index - Logarithmic key lookups

File Growth Strategy

The file grows in fixed-size blocks (aligned allocation), avoiding the exponential doubling of traditional databases. Waste is bounded to at most one block regardless of database size:

// PageFileConfig presets
PageFileConfig.Small   // 8 KB pages,  grow by  512 KB
PageFileConfig.Default // 16 KB pages, grow by    1 MB
PageFileConfig.Large   // 32 KB pages, grow by    2 MB

Page Structure

// Page Header (32 bytes)
struct PageHeader
{
    uint PageId;
    ushort PageType;  // Data, Index, Free
    ushort FreeSpace;
    uint NextPageId;
    // ...
}

C-BSON Format

BLite uses C-BSON (Compressed BSON) - a space-efficient variant that achieves 30-60% size reduction through field name compression:

Key Compression

Standard BSON stores field names as null-terminated strings in every document. C-BSON replaces them with 2-byte numeric field IDs:

// Standard BSON element
[1 byte: type][N bytes: "field_name\0"][value]

// C-BSON element (compressed)
[1 byte: type][2 bytes: field_id][value]

Schema-Based Mapping

Field IDs are assigned via a schema dictionary:

  • "_id" → Field ID 1 (2 bytes instead of 4)
  • "email" → Field ID 2 (2 bytes instead of 6)
  • "created_at" → Field ID 3 (2 bytes instead of 11)

Benefits

  • 30-60% smaller documents - Less storage and faster I/O
  • Zero allocation - Direct Span<byte> operations
  • BSON type compatible - Same type codes and value encoding
  • Schema evolution - Add/remove fields safely

Transaction Flow

1. BeginTransaction()
   └─> Allocate transaction ID
   └─> Create WAL entry (BEGIN)

2. Write Operations
   └─> Write to WAL (uncommitted)
   └─> Cache in memory

3. Commit()
   └─> Write WAL commit marker
   └─> Flush to PageFile
   └─> Clear WAL cache

4. Rollback() (if needed)
   └─> Discard WAL entries
   └─> Restore from checkpoint

Indexing Strategies

B-Tree Index

Used for:

  • Primary key lookups
  • Range queries (>, <, BETWEEN)
  • Prefix searches (StartsWith)

R-Tree Index

Used for:

  • Geospatial queries
  • Bounding box searches
  • Nearest neighbor (k-NN)

Memory Management

BLite minimizes garbage collection pressure:

  • ✅ stackalloc for temporary buffers
  • ✅ ArrayPool for reusable arrays
  • ✅ Memory-mapped files for OS-level caching
  • ✅ Span<T> for zero-copy operations

Concurrency Model

BLite supports multi-threading within a single process:

  • ✅ Multiple readers simultaneously
  • ✅ Single writer at a time (per transaction)
  • ✅ Snapshot isolation for consistency
  • ⚠️ Not designed for multi-process access

Performance Characteristics

OperationComplexityNotes
InsertO(log n)B-Tree index insert
FindByIdO(log n)Primary key lookup
Range QueryO(log n + k)k = result size
Spatial QueryO(log n + k)R-Tree search
Full ScanO(n)No index available

Crash Recovery

BLite uses WAL for durability:

// On database open
if (WAL.Exists())
{
    // Replay uncommitted transactions
    WAL.Recover();
    
    // Apply committed writes
    WAL.Flush();
    
    // Clear WAL
    WAL.Truncate();
}

Hot Backup

BLite supports online, consistent backups without stopping the engine. Concurrent reads and writes remain safe during the copy:

// 1. Checkpoint WAL into PageFile (under commit lock)
// 2. Copy PageFile to destination (while holding the lock)
// 3. Release lock — normal writes resume

// The WAL is NOT copied — the destination is already consistent

See Transactions → Hot Backup for the user-facing API.

Design Philosophy

  • 🎯 Performance First - Zero allocation wherever possible
  • 🛡️ ACID Compliance - Full transaction support
  • 🚀 Modern .NET - Leverage Span<T>, Memory<T>
  • 🔧 Developer Experience - LINQ, attributes, source generators
  • 📦 Embedded First - No external dependencies

© 2026 BLite Project. Open Source (MIT).

"Sky is Blite, no Clouds today."
